text: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. The glass left a wet stain on the tile floor.
The cat jumped onto the kitchen counter. The cat knocked over a glass jar. The cat leaped down to the floor. The cat hid under the dining table.
Ben opened the old wooden gate. Ben walked into the garden. Ben picked a ripe red tomato. Ben placed the tomato in a wicker basket.
The snowball rolled down the steep hill. The snowball gathered more snow along the way. The snowball grew to the size of a boulder. The snowball crashed into a wooden fence.
Clara lit a small match. Clara held the match to the candle wick. Clara watched the flame grow bright. Clara blew out the match with a quick breath.
The balloon floated up from the child's hand. The balloon drifted over the rooftops. The balloon caught on a sharp tree branch. The balloon popped with a loud bang.
David picked up the heavy axe. David swung the axe at the fallen log. David split the log in half. David stacked the two pieces near the fireplace.
The river rose after the heavy rain. The river flooded the nearby meadow. The river swept away a small wooden bridge. The river left thick mud across the farmland.
Emma opened the dusty attic door. Emma found an old leather suitcase inside the attic. Emma unlatched the suitcase carefully. Emma discovered a bundle of yellowed letters inside the suitcase.
The egg rolled off the kitchen counter. The egg fell onto the hard tile floor. The egg cracked open on impact. The egg left a sticky mess on the tiles.
Frank loaded the fishing gear into the truck. Frank drove the truck to the lake. Frank parked the truck near the dock. Frank unloaded the fishing gear onto the wooden dock.
The kite caught a strong gust of wind. The kite soared high above the park. The kite tangled around a power line. The kite ripped apart in the wind.
Grace dug a small hole in the flowerbed. Grace dropped a tulip bulb into the hole. Grace covered the bulb with dark soil. Grace watered the soil with a green can.
The match struck against the rough box. The match burst into a tiny flame. The match burned down toward the fingertips. The match went out in a thin wisp of smoke.
Henry carried the ladder to the side of the house. Henry leaned the ladder against the gutter. Henry climbed the ladder to the roof. Henry cleared the dead leaves from the gutter.
The stone skipped across the surface of the pond. The stone bounced three times on the water. The stone lost speed near the far bank. The stone sank to the bottom of the pond.
Iris poured flour into a large mixing bowl. Iris added two eggs to the flour. Iris stirred the mixture with a wooden spoon. Iris poured the batter into a baking pan.
The candle burned on the windowsill all evening. The candle melted down to a small stub. The candle dripped hot wax onto the sill. The candle flickered out in a draft from the window.
Jack spotted a deer at the edge of the forest. Jack raised the camera slowly. Jack pressed the shutter button without a sound. Jack captured a clear photograph of the deer.
The boat drifted away from the wooden dock. The boat floated out toward the center of the lake. The boat rocked gently in the breeze. The boat bumped against a cluster of reeds on the far shore.
Kate pulled a dusty book from the top shelf. Kate blew the dust off the cover. Kate opened the book to the first page. Kate began reading the faded handwritten text.
The leaf detached from the top of the oak tree. The leaf drifted slowly through the cool air. The leaf landed on the surface of the pond. The leaf floated across the pond toward the bank.
Leo tossed the ball high into the air. Leo swung the bat with both hands. Leo struck the ball with a sharp crack. Leo watched the ball fly over the fence.
The rope frayed against the sharp edge of the rock. The rope snapped under the heavy weight. The rope fell into the ravine below. The rope landed in a coil on the rocks.
Maria pulled the suitcase from the closet. Maria packed three dresses into the suitcase. Maria zipped the suitcase shut. Maria carried the suitcase down the stairs to the front door.
The wheel hit a deep pothole in the road. The wheel bent out of shape on impact. The wheel made a grinding noise against the frame. The wheel came loose from the axle.
Noah scooped sand into a plastic bucket. Noah flipped the bucket upside down on the beach. Noah lifted the bucket away carefully. Noah admired the small sand tower on the beach.
The bird swooped down from the tall pine tree. The bird snatched a worm from the wet grass. The bird flew back up to the pine tree. The bird fed the worm to the chicks in the nest.
Olivia picked up a smooth flat stone. Olivia wound back the stone in a swift motion. Olivia released the stone across the lake. Olivia counted four skips before the stone sank.
The branch cracked under the weight of the snow. The branch broke away from the trunk of the oak. The branch crashed down onto the garden path. The branch blocked the entire path to the shed.
Paul measured two planks of wood with a tape measure. Paul sawed the planks to equal lengths. Paul nailed the planks together at a right angle. Paul sanded the rough edges of the joined planks.
The fire started in a pile of dry leaves. The fire spread quickly across the hillside. The fire consumed a row of small bushes. The fire burned out at the edge of the dirt road.
Rachel placed the kettle on the stove. Rachel turned the burner to high heat. Rachel waited for the water to boil. Rachel poured the boiling water into a ceramic mug.
The ball bounced off the backboard above the rim. The ball circled the rim twice. The ball teetered on the edge for a moment. The ball dropped through the net.
Sam climbed the steep rocky trail. Sam reached the summit before noon. Sam planted a small flag in the ground at the top. Sam sat on a flat rock and ate a sandwich.
The door swung open in a gust of wind. The door slammed against the wall. The door cracked the plaster behind the door handle. The door bounced back halfway shut.
Tina placed a blank canvas on the easel. Tina squeezed blue paint onto the palette. Tina dipped a wide brush into the paint. Tina swept a bold blue stripe across the canvas.
The truck rumbled down the gravel road. The truck hit a bump near the bridge. The truck lost a crate from the open bed. The truck left the crate lying on the road behind.
Victor removed the flat tire from the car. Victor placed the spare tire on the axle. Victor tightened the bolts with a wrench. Victor lowered the car back down to the ground.
The wave built up far from the shore. The wave rose to a steep crest near the beach. The wave crashed onto the sandy shore. The wave dragged a child's sandcastle into the water.
Wendy threaded a needle with red thread. Wendy pushed the needle through the torn fabric. Wendy pulled the thread tight with each stitch. Wendy tied a small knot at the end of the seam.
The arrow left the bow at full draw. The arrow sailed across the open field. The arrow struck the outer ring of the target. The arrow lodged deep into the hay bale behind the target.
Arthur polished the old wooden desk with a rag. Arthur applied a coat of varnish to the surface. Arthur let the varnish dry in the sun. Arthur buffed the desk to a smooth shine.

The train pulled away from the station platform. The train picked up speed through the countryside. The train crossed a long iron bridge over the river. The train slowed to a stop at a small rural station.
Beth scooped a handful of clay from the bucket. Beth shaped the clay into a small bowl on the wheel. Beth smoothed the rim of the bowl with wet fingers. Beth placed the bowl on a shelf to dry.
The coin flew up from the flick of a thumb. The coin spun in the air above the table. The coin landed on the wooden table with a clink. The coin wobbled and settled on heads.

Carl pushed the canoe into the shallow water. Carl climbed into the canoe from the sandy bank. Carl paddled the canoe along the quiet stream. Carl steered the canoe around a fallen tree in the stream.
The vase tipped over on the narrow shelf. The vase rolled toward the edge of the shelf. The vase fell off the shelf and hit the floor. The vase shattered into dozens of ceramic pieces.
Diana unwrapped the brown paper parcel on the table. Diana lifted the lid off the cardboard box inside. Diana pulled out a folded wool blanket. Diana spread the blanket across the back of the couch.
The fence leaned after the strong windstorm. The fence lost three boards from the damaged section. The fence sagged lower under the weight of the vine. The fence collapsed flat onto the ground.
Edgar swept the sawdust off the workshop floor. Edgar collected the sawdust into a metal dustpan. Edgar carried the dustpan to the bin outside. Edgar dumped the sawdust into the compost bin.
The puddle formed in a dip in the sidewalk. The puddle spread wider after the second downpour. The puddle soaked the shoes of a passing jogger. The puddle dried up in the afternoon sun.
Fiona picked a lock of wool from the basket. Fiona fed the wool onto the spinning wheel. Fiona spun the wool into a thin strand. Fiona wound the strand around a wooden bobbin.
The spark jumped from the campfire to the dry grass. The spark ignited a small circle of dry grass. The spark grew into a ring of low flames. The spark burned out at the edge of the sandy clearing.
George pulled a weed from the vegetable garden. George tossed the weed into a wheelbarrow. George rolled the wheelbarrow to the compost heap. George emptied the wheelbarrow onto the heap.
The marble rolled off the top step of the staircase. The marble bounced down each step with a click. The marble reached the bottom of the staircase. The marble spun to a stop on the hallway floor.
Hannah opened the oven door and felt a rush of heat. Hannah slid the baking tray into the hot oven. Hannah closed the oven door with a padded mitt. Hannah set the kitchen timer on the counter.
The cloud moved in from the western mountains. The cloud darkened the sky over the valley. The cloud released a heavy burst of rain. The cloud passed beyond the eastern ridge within the hour.
Ian removed the old wallpaper strip by strip. Ian scraped the glue residue from the bare wall. Ian applied a smooth coat of primer to the wall. Ian rolled pale green paint over the primed surface.
The acorn fell from the tall oak in the yard. The acorn rolled into a crack in the soil. The acorn split open after several weeks of rain. The acorn sent a tiny green shoot up through the dirt.
Julia placed a mousetrap under the kitchen sink. Julia baited the trap with a small piece of cheese. Julia heard a snap during the night. Julia found the trap sprung the following morning.

The chain rattled loose from the rusty bolt. The chain slid off the edge of the loading platform. The chain dropped into the mud below the platform. The chain coiled in a heap at the base of the wall.
Kevin opened the paint can with a flathead screwdriver. Kevin stirred the thick white paint with a stick. Kevin dipped a roller into the paint tray. Kevin pressed the roller against the bare drywall.

The icicle grew longer on the roof's edge all winter. The icicle glistened in the pale morning sunlight. The icicle cracked at the base during a warm afternoon. The icicle fell and stuck point-first into the snowbank.
Laura placed a tea bag in a porcelain cup. Laura poured hot water over the tea bag. Laura let the tea steep for several minutes. Laura removed the tea bag with a small spoon.
The sled started at the top of the snowy slope. The sled gained speed on the steep middle section. The sled flew over a small bump near the bottom. The sled slid to a stop in the flat field.
Marcus hammered a nail into the wooden post. Marcus hung a birdhouse on the nail. Marcus filled the birdhouse with sunflower seeds. Marcus stepped back to watch from the porch.
The ink spread across the white cotton tablecloth. The ink soaked deep into the fabric. The ink left a dark blue stain on the tablecloth. The ink ruined the center of the tablecloth.

Nina laid fresh bricks along the garden border. Nina pressed each brick into the damp soil. Nina tapped each brick level with a rubber mallet. Nina filled the gaps between the bricks with loose gravel.
The pipe froze during the cold snap overnight. The pipe expanded from the pressure of the ice inside. The pipe burst open at a weak joint. The pipe sprayed water across the basement floor.
Oscar baited a hook with a small earthworm. Oscar cast the fishing line into the river. Oscar felt a sharp tug on the line. Oscar reeled in a silver trout from the river.
The paper airplane sailed off the rooftop. The paper airplane glided over the parking lot. The paper airplane dipped lower near the fence. The paper airplane landed in a puddle on the sidewalk.
Phoebe unlocked the garden shed with a brass key. Phoebe pulled out the old push mower. Phoebe rolled the mower to the front lawn. Phoebe mowed the lawn in even rows.
The log shifted in the fireplace. The log crumbled into glowing embers. The log sent a shower of sparks up the chimney. The log collapsed into a heap of white ash.

Quinn carried the heavy sandbag to the riverbank. Quinn dropped the sandbag into a gap in the barrier. Quinn pressed the sandbag flat with both hands. Quinn reinforced the wall with a second sandbag on top.
The hose connected to the spigot on the shed wall. The hose filled with water when the spigot opened. The hose stiffened from the pressure. The hose sprayed a wide arc of water over the garden.
Rosa mixed cement powder and water in a bucket. Rosa spread the wet cement along the crack in the wall. Rosa smoothed the cement with a flat trowel. Rosa left the cement to harden overnight.
The tire blew out on the highway shoulder. The tire shredded into strips of black rubber. The tire scattered rubber debris across the lane. The tire left a dark skid mark on the pavement.
Simon opened the mailbox at the end of the driveway. Simon pulled out a thick brown envelope. Simon tore open the envelope at the kitchen table. Simon read the letter inside the envelope twice.
The dam cracked during the spring thaw. The dam leaked a thin stream of water through the crack. The dam gave way under the force of the flood. The dam released a wall of water into the valley.
Ursula placed a glass jar under the dripping faucet. Ursula waited for the jar to fill with water. Ursula carried the full jar to the windowsill. Ursula dropped a small plant cutting into the jar.

The pillar crumbled at the base after the earthquake. The pillar tilted to one side above the rubble. The pillar broke apart into large stone blocks. The pillar fell across the entrance of the old temple.
Wallace pulled the cork from the glass bottle. Wallace poured dark red juice into a tall glass. Wallace set the bottle back on the counter. Wallace carried the glass to the front porch.
The tent pole bent under the heavy snowfall. The tent pole snapped in the middle with a crack. The tent pole tore through the canvas roof. The tent pole brought the entire tent down flat.
Yvonne turned the key in the car's ignition. Yvonne backed the car out of the narrow garage. Yvonne shifted the car into drive on the street. Yvonne steered the car onto the main road.
The nail loosened from the plank after years of weather. The nail slid halfway out of the old plank. The nail caught on the sole of a passing shoe. The nail bent sideways against the warped plank.
Alan peeled the label off the empty jam jar. Alan rinsed the jar under cold tap water. Alan dried the jar with a cotton towel. Alan placed the clean jar in a row on the pantry shelf.
The avalanche started near the rocky peak. The avalanche swept down the steep mountainside. The avalanche buried the trail under deep snow. The avalanche knocked over a row of pine trees at the base.
Brenda unzipped the sleeping bag on the tent floor. Brenda crawled inside the sleeping bag. Brenda pulled the zipper up to the chin. Brenda rested against the rolled-up jacket used as a pillow.

The lamp toppled off the nightstand during the tremor. The lamp hit the hardwood floor with a thud. The lamp cracked at the base of the ceramic stand. The lamp went dark when the bulb shattered inside.

Calvin placed the fishing net at the edge of the dock. Calvin lowered the net into the shallow bay water. Calvin waited until a school of minnows swam over the net. Calvin lifted the net full of minnows out of the water.

The compass needle spun inside the brass casing. The compass needle steadied toward the north marking. The compass needle guided the hikers through the dense fog. The compass needle pointed the way to the ridge trail.

Donna pressed the dough flat on a floured board. Donna cut round shapes from the dough with a tin cutter. Donna placed the dough rounds on a greased baking sheet. Donna slid the baking sheet into the hot oven.

The plank snapped under the weight of the heavy barrel. The plank dropped the barrel into the cellar below. The plank left a jagged hole in the floor. The plank hung in two pieces from the broken beam.

Elliot pried the lid off the old wooden crate. Elliot removed a layer of straw packing from inside the crate. Elliot lifted a wrapped porcelain plate from the crate. Elliot set the plate gently on the dining table.

The tire swing hung from a thick rope on the elm tree. The tire swing rocked in the strong autumn wind. The tire swing twisted the rope into a tight spiral. The tire swing unwound and spun in fast circles.

Frances dropped a pebble into the deep well. Frances heard the pebble bounce off the stone walls. Frances counted four seconds of silence after the last bounce. Frances heard a faint splash echo from the bottom of the well.

The shutter banged against the house in the storm. The shutter cracked along a row of wooden slats. The shutter tore loose from the upper hinge. The shutter dangled by the lower hinge against the brick wall.
Graham placed a log on the chopping block. Graham raised the splitting maul above the log. Graham drove the maul down through the center of the log. Graham kicked the two halves aside into a pile.

The rowboat scraped against the gravel shore. The rowboat slid halfway up onto the dry bank. The rowboat tipped sideways and spilled the oars. The rowboat came to rest against a large flat rock on the bank.
The library lent a heavy atlas. The library hosted a chess tournament. The library displayed old framed photographs. The library received a donated wooden shelf from a volunteer.
The bakery sold rye bread every Saturday. The bakery donated leftover rolls to a food bank. The bakery won a prize at the county fair. The bakery hired a new pastry decorator.
Marcus climbed a tall cedar tree in the meadow. Marcus repaired a broken lock on the barn door. Marcus painted a wooden birdhouse bright yellow. Marcus carried a heavy bag of grain to the shed.

The museum displayed a Viking longship replica. The museum printed new brochures for the summer exhibit. The museum replaced the carpet in the east wing. The museum hosted a pottery workshop for children.
Nora fixed a flat tire on a gravel road. Nora assembled a bookcase without any instructions. Nora mailed a thick envelope to a cousin overseas. Nora planted basil seeds in a clay pot on the balcony.

The school replaced the flag on the front pole. The school donated old textbooks to a community center. The school repaved the basketball court in the yard. The school installed a new water fountain in the hallway.

Oliver carved a small owl from a block of pine. Oliver washed the windows on the south side of the house. Oliver replaced a burned-out bulb in the porch light. Oliver stacked firewood behind the garden shed.

The restaurant served grilled trout on Friday evenings. The restaurant replaced the old neon sign above the door. The restaurant planted rosemary bushes near the patio. The restaurant framed a review clipping on the wall.
Penny baked a loaf of sourdough bread. Penny sealed the driveway cracks with black tar. Penny hung a framed map of Iceland in the hallway. Penny sharpened every kitchen knife with a whetstone.

The hospital purchased a new emergency generator. The hospital planted a row of birch trees near the entrance. The hospital donated surplus blankets to a shelter. The hospital repainted the waiting room walls light blue.

Roger built a stone wall along the property line. Roger replaced the kitchen faucet with a brass one. Roger waxed the hardwood floors in the living room. Roger tuned the old piano in the front parlor.

The hotel replaced the lobby carpet with marble tile. The hotel hung landscape paintings in the hallway. The hotel donated old linens to a local shelter. The hotel installed a fountain in the courtyard garden.

Sandra translated a poem from French into English. Sandra patched a hole in the garden fence. Sandra donated a box of canned goods to a food drive. Sandra cleaned the rain gutters on the east side of the roof.

The farm harvested a large crop of sweet potatoes. The farm built a new chicken coop near the barn. The farm sold goat cheese at the weekend market. The farm repaired the windmill on the south pasture.

Thomas replaced the shingles on the garage roof. Thomas donated a rocking chair to a thrift store. Thomas polished the brass knocker on the front door. Thomas planted a row of sunflowers along the fence.

The factory produced a limited run of glass bottles. The factory donated surplus uniforms to a vocational school. The factory installed a new conveyor belt in the east wing. The factory repainted the loading dock bright yellow.

Ursula knitted a wool scarf during a long train ride. Ursula refinished an oak dresser with dark stain. Ursula mailed a birthday card to a friend in Oslo. Ursula pressed wildflowers inside a heavy dictionary.

The theater staged a one-act comedy in the spring. The theater replaced the velvet curtain on the main stage. The theater donated old costumes to a drama club. The theater installed new speakers in the balcony section.

Vincent repaired a cracked window in the attic. Vincent caught a large bass at the reservoir. Vincent oiled the hinges on every door in the house. Vincent framed a pencil sketch of the old covered bridge.

The park installed a wooden bench near the pond. The park cleared a new hiking trail through the birch grove. The park hosted a kite festival on a windy weekend. The park planted wildflowers along the eastern path.

Wanda organized a box of loose photographs by location. Wanda returned three overdue books to the public library. Wanda replaced the broken latch on the mailbox. Wanda sewed a button back onto a winter coat.

The church replaced the stained glass panel above the altar. The church donated canned soup to a neighborhood pantry. The church repaired the stone steps at the main entrance. The church hosted a choral concert on a rainy evening.

Xavier cleaned the chimney flue with a long wire brush. Xavier patched a leak in the basement ceiling. Xavier carved a walking stick from a fallen maple branch. Xavier pickled a jar of green tomatoes from the garden.

The zoo built a new habitat for the river otters. The zoo donated old veterinary equipment to a wildlife clinic. The zoo printed a colorful map for visitors. The zoo planted bamboo along the panda enclosure.

Yolanda tuned the guitar with a clip-on tuner. Yolanda sorted a drawer of old coins by country. Yolanda replaced the worn brake pads on the bicycle. Yolanda hemmed a pair of linen curtains for the kitchen window.

The post office sold a commemorative stamp series. The post office installed a wheelchair ramp at the side entrance. The post office replaced the clock above the service counter. The post office donated old mailbags to a craft workshop.

Angela repaired a torn page in a leather-bound journal. Angela swept the front porch after a dust storm. Angela polished the silver candlesticks on the mantle. Angela stacked clean towels on the bathroom shelf.

The fire station washed the oldest truck in the fleet. The fire station hung a banner across the front entrance. The fire station replaced the alarm bell with an electronic siren. The fire station donated old helmets to a safety exhibit.
Brian filed the rough edge of a metal bracket. Brian hung a hammock between two oak trees. Brian sealed the deck boards with a clear coat. Brian wound the grandfather clock in the front hallway.

The airport installed new seating in the departure lounge. The airport donated lost luggage items to a charity auction. The airport resurfaced the shorter of the two runways. The airport planted low hedges along the arrival walkway.

Celia peeled apples for a deep-dish pie. Celia stitched a torn seam on a canvas tote bag. Celia replaced the batteries in the hallway smoke detector. Celia labeled a row of spice jars with a felt-tip pen.

The lighthouse received a fresh coat of white paint. The lighthouse stored a backup lamp in the supply room. The lighthouse logged a passing cargo ship in the night record. The lighthouse flew a red pennant from the railing on a windy day.

Derek sanded a rough plank for the bookshelf project. Derek sharpened the garden shears behind the garage. Derek taped a torn corner on an old road map. Derek swept broken glass from the workshop floor.

The harbor launched a new patrol boat in the spring. The harbor replaced the wooden pilings along the south dock. The harbor hosted a model sailboat race for children. The harbor donated old rope to a maritime museum.
Elena organized the spice rack in alphabetical order. Elena replaced a cracked tile in the bathroom. Elena oiled the squeaky hinge on the back gate. Elena darned a wool sock with a thick needle.

The stadium installed floodlights above the north bleachers. The stadium resurfaced the running track with new rubber. The stadium donated old metal bleachers to a community field. The stadium painted fresh boundary lines on the grass.

Felix welded a broken hinge on the iron gate. Felix cleaned the fish tank filter in the living room. Felix tightened the loose railing on the back stairs. Felix sewed a leather patch onto a canvas jacket.

The clinic replaced the ceiling tiles in the waiting area. The clinic donated a wheelchair to a rehabilitation center. The clinic printed new intake forms for the front desk. The clinic installed a water cooler near the entrance.

Georgia pressed a four-leaf clover inside a notebook. Georgia sealed a crack in the basement wall with caulk. Georgia rehung the crooked painting above the mantel. Georgia braided garlic bulbs into a string for the pantry.

The bridge received new steel cables on the east side. The bridge bore the weight of a passing freight convoy. The bridge displayed a bronze plaque honoring the original builder. The bridge carried a water pipe beneath the roadway deck.

Harold replaced the spark plugs in the old tractor. Harold shelved a collection of bird guides by region. Harold nailed a horseshoe above the barn door for luck. Harold stacked clay pots under the greenhouse bench.

The gallery exhibited a series of charcoal portraits. The gallery sold handmade ceramic vases in the gift shop. The gallery hosted an opening reception with live violin music. The gallery replaced the track lighting on the north wall.

Ingrid folded paper cranes for a charity fundraiser. Ingrid replaced the worn welcome mat on the front porch. Ingrid polished the copper kettle until the copper gleamed. Ingrid filed old tax receipts into a labeled binder.

The warehouse stored crates of dried pasta on the top shelf. The warehouse replaced a cracked window on the west wall. The warehouse loaned two forklifts to a neighboring business. The warehouse hosted an inventory training session for new staff.

James glued a broken handle back onto the coffee mug. James retied the laces on a pair of hiking boots. James wiped down the dusty blinds in the bedroom. James tightened a loose screw on the cabinet door.

The bookstore arranged a window display of travel memoirs. The bookstore donated unsold paperbacks to a prison library. The bookstore offered free bookmarks at the register. The bookstore replaced the awning above the front entrance.

Kara sketched a portrait of the old stone mill. Kara organized fishing lures into a divided tackle box. Kara tightened the spokes on the front wheel of the bicycle. Kara labeled storage bins in the basement with a marker.

The cabin stored split firewood under the back porch. The cabin displayed a hand-drawn map of the surrounding trails. The cabin kept a first-aid kit in the kitchen cupboard. The cabin featured a stone hearth in the main room.
Liam sanded the rust off an old garden trowel. Liam rolled fresh paint onto the hallway ceiling. Liam sorted a jar of assorted screws by size. Liam taped a wildlife calendar to the shed wall.

The inn served warm cider in the front parlor. The inn placed fresh lavender sachets in each guest room. The inn displayed a hand-stitched quilt in the lobby. The inn kept a leather guest book on the reception desk.
Martha trimmed the hedges along the driveway. Martha replaced a missing button on a flannel shirt. Martha sharpened a set of colored pencils. Martha wound a ball of twine for the garden.

The monastery bottled honey from the apiary. The monastery repaired a cracked stone bench in the courtyard. The monastery shelved a donated collection of hymn books. The monastery hung a brass bell above the refectory door.

Nathan stained the fence posts a deep walnut color. Nathan sharpened the blade of the hand saw. Nathan glued a loose sole back onto a leather boot. Nathan dusted the picture frames in the living room.

The observatory recorded the brightness of a distant star. The observatory published a chart of the winter constellations. The observatory cleaned the primary lens of the main telescope. The observatory hosted a group of visiting geology students.
Opal braided a leather strap for a wristband. Opal rinsed mud from a pair of rubber boots. Opal labeled a set of mason jars with harvest dates. Opal stacked clean plates on the wooden drying rack.

The ranch mended a section of barbed wire along the ridge. The ranch stored bales of hay in the east barn. The ranch branded the newest calves in the spring pen. The ranch replaced the water trough near the corral gate.

Patrick peeled the bark off a birch branch for kindling. Patrick leveled the legs of a wobbly kitchen table. Patrick refilled the bird feeder hanging from the porch beam. Patrick swept the ashes out of the fireplace.

The pier replaced several rotting boards on the walkway. The pier sold bait and tackle from a small wooden booth. The pier displayed a catch-of-the-day chalkboard sign. The pier stored life jackets in a weatherproof locker.

Rebecca ironed a linen tablecloth for the dining room. Rebecca wound the mantle clock with a small brass key. Rebecca arranged dried herbs in glass jars on the counter. Rebecca reinforced the handle of a woven market basket.

The mill ground a bushel of winter wheat into coarse flour. The mill replaced a worn leather belt on the main drive wheel. The mill donated surplus grain sacks to a local feed store. The mill recorded the day's output in a cloth-bound ledger.

Sean polished a pair of leather dress shoes. Sean caulked the seam around the bathroom tub. Sean hung a row of tools on pegboard hooks in the garage. Sean rolled up the garden hose after watering the lawn.

The chapel lit beeswax candles on the carved stone altar. The chapel received a hand-woven rug from a visiting artisan. The chapel stored extra hymnals in a cedar chest. The chapel displayed a donated oil painting of the valley.

Teresa folded a stack of clean laundry on the bed. Teresa replaced the filter in the kitchen range hood. Teresa latched the garden gate after the dog wandered out. Teresa pinned a recipe card to the corkboard by the stove.

The sawmill stacked fresh-cut lumber in the drying yard. The sawmill replaced a dull blade on the main circular saw. The sawmill donated wood scraps to a school art class. The sawmill logged the delivery weights in a binder.

Ulrich bound a torn book spine with cloth tape. Ulrich raked the gravel path around the herb garden. Ulrich replaced a cracked pane in the greenhouse wall. Ulrich oiled the chain on the old well pulley.

The vineyard pruned the oldest vines on the hillside. The vineyard bottled a small batch of red wine. The vineyard repaired the stone wall along the northern terrace. The vineyard hosted a group of visiting sommeliers.

Vera stenciled a pattern of ivy leaves on the nursery wall. Vera replaced the worn latch on the jewelry box. Vera sorted buttons into separate tins by color. Vera folded a quilted blanket at the foot of the guest bed.

The market sold fresh pears from a local orchard. The market displayed jars of raw honey on the corner table. The market offered woven baskets at a craft stall. The market collected canned goods for a winter food drive.

Wallace mounted a smoke detector on the bedroom ceiling. Wallace repaired a frayed rug with a heavy needle and thread. Wallace replaced the gasket on the pressure cooker lid. Wallace labeled the fuse switches in the breaker panel.

The cottage stored jars of blackberry jam in the cellar. The cottage displayed a barometer on the front porch wall. The cottage kept a stack of wool blankets in the linen closet. The cottage hung copper pots above the kitchen hearth.

Xavier restrung an old acoustic guitar with nylon strings. Xavier scraped peeling paint from the window frames. Xavier leveled the flagstones on the garden path. Xavier rolled a fresh coat of sealant onto the deck boards.

The dairy farm churned fresh butter in a wooden barrel. The dairy farm delivered glass bottles of milk to the village. The dairy farm repaired the gate on the milking parlor. The dairy farm hosted a school group for a morning tour.

Yvonne stitched a torn pocket on a denim jacket. Yvonne arranged river stones around the base of the fountain. Yvonne cleaned the clay pots stored under the potting bench. Yvonne fastened a loose bracket on the curtain rod.

The forge shaped a set of iron hooks for the kitchen rack. The forge repaired a cracked anvil stand with oak planks. The forge donated decorative hinges to a historical society. The forge tempered a custom blade for a woodworking chisel.

Zara cleaned the lens of the old film camera. Zara replaced the felt pads on the dining chair legs. Zara organized seed packets by planting season. Zara taped a loose corner of wallpaper in the entryway.

The orchard grafted new branches onto the old apple trees. The orchard sold wooden crates of plums at the roadside stand. The orchard repaired the irrigation line near the peach rows. The orchard donated bruised fruit to a cider press.

Adam laid fresh straw in the stable stalls. Adam repaired a cracked handle on the wheelbarrow. Adam hung a thermometer inside the greenhouse door. Adam sorted nails into labeled coffee cans on the shelf.

The distillery aged barrels of rye whiskey in a stone cellar. The distillery replaced the copper tubing on the smallest still. The distillery donated oak barrel halves to a garden center. The distillery printed new labels for the seasonal blend.

Bianca waxed the runners of an antique rocking chair. Bianca replaced a frayed pull cord on the attic light. Bianca dried rosemary sprigs on a wire rack in the kitchen. Bianca polished the glass face of the hallway barometer.

The marina repainted the hull of the harbor patrol boat. The marina replaced the dock cleats on the south pier. The marina donated old life preservers to a sailing school. The marina stored spare anchors in the equipment shed.

Conrad sharpened a chisel on a flat whetstone. Conrad measured and cut a length of copper pipe. Conrad sewed a canvas cover for the patio table. Conrad stacked reclaimed bricks behind the potting shed.

The lodge hung antler chandeliers in the main dining hall. The lodge stocked firewood in a covered bin near the lobby. The lodge served wild mushroom soup on autumn evenings. The lodge replaced the worn boot mat at the front entrance.

Daphne carved a wooden spoon from a cherry branch. Daphne sealed the stone patio with a clear waterproof coat. Daphne sorted old magazines into boxes for recycling. Daphne replaced the washer in the dripping kitchen tap.

The foundry cast a bronze plaque for the town memorial. The foundry donated scrap metal to an art workshop. The foundry replaced the brick lining inside the main furnace. The foundry stored sand molds on steel shelving units.

Evan strung new wire between the fence posts. Evan cleaned moss off the stone bench in the side garden. Evan reattached a loose handle on the shed door. Evan folded a canvas tarp over the woodpile before the rain.

The granary weighed sacks of barley on a floor scale. The granary repaired the sliding door on the loading bay. The granary donated surplus oats to a horse rescue. The granary swept the concrete floor after the morning delivery.

Faye polished the brass fittings on the front door. Faye replaced the cracked soap dish in the bathroom. Faye organized paintbrushes by width in a metal tin. Faye hung a set of dried chili peppers from a kitchen hook.

The apiary harvested honeycomb from the strongest hive. The apiary planted a border of clover around the bee yard. The apiary repaired the mesh on two inspection veils. The apiary donated beeswax blocks to a candle maker.

Gordon latched the tool chest in the back of the truck. Gordon scrubbed engine grease from a set of wrenches. Gordon patched a torn knee on a pair of work trousers. Gordon filed a rough burr off the edge of a steel plate.

The creamery pressed a wheel of aged cheddar cheese. The creamery replaced the rubber gaskets on the pasteurizer. The creamery donated sample trays to a harvest festival. The creamery recorded milk volumes in a wall-mounted logbook.

Helen cataloged a box of old postcards by country. Helen scrubbed the birdbath in the center of the garden. Helen replaced a broken clasp on a leather handbag. Helen rolled a fresh sheet of beeswax onto a candle wick.

The smokehouse hung racks of cured salmon from ceiling hooks. The smokehouse repaired the vent flap on the north wall. The smokehouse donated hickory chips to a barbecue competition. The smokehouse stacked alder logs beside the firebox.

Ian fitted new glass panes into the cold frame lid. Ian repointed the mortar between the chimney bricks. Ian labeled a shelf of homemade preserves with the harvest year. Ian coiled a length of copper wire into the toolbox.

The boatyard sanded the keel of a wooden sloop. The boatyard replaced frayed dock lines on the rental fleet. The boatyard donated an old anchor to a seafood restaurant. The boatyard painted the name on the stern of a fishing trawler.

Judith wove a reed basket for the farmers market stall. Judith replaced the wick in an old kerosene lantern. Judith pressed autumn leaves between sheets of wax paper. Judith tightened the hinges on the cellar trapdoor.

The tannery stretched a cowhide on a large wooden frame. The tannery donated leather scraps to a local cobbler. The tannery replaced the drain grate in the soaking pit. The tannery stamped a maker's mark on a finished belt.

The windmill pumped water into the stone irrigation channel. The windmill stored spare canvas sails in the ground-floor storeroom. The windmill displayed a brass plaque honoring the original miller. The windmill ground a sack of rye grain into dark flour.

Keith replaced the worn leather strap on a saddlebag. Keith cleaned the soot from the inside of the wood stove. Keith mended a tear in the mosquito screen on the porch door. Keith hung a bundle of dried lavender in the closet.

The cannery labeled tin cans of sardines with red ink. The cannery donated a pallet of canned tomatoes to a food bank. The cannery repaired the steam valve on the main boiler. The cannery stored empty glass jars on wooden pallets in the warehouse.

Lydia sanded the top of an old pine dresser. Lydia replaced the zipper on a canvas duffle bag. Lydia watered the ferns hanging from the front porch rafters. Lydia organized recipe cards into a small wooden filing box.
